sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Shentel's Employee Volunteer Initiative Gives Back to Local Charities
feed.summary: Shenandoah Telecommunications Company (Shentel) announced that 114 employees volunteered over 572 hours in the past year, supporting charities focused on food and housing insecurity. Through initiatives like the Summer Backpack Program and The Big Give, Shentel and its employees donated over $48,000, including matching contributions, to local organizations across several states. This effort highlights Shentel's commitment to community service, empowering employees through paid volunteer time to make a meaningful local impact.

feed.title: Perion Network: Q4 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: Perion Network Ltd. (PERI) reported a profit of $8 million in its fourth quarter, translating to adjusted earnings of 49 cents per share, surpassing analyst expectations for revenue. The digital media company posted revenues of $137.1 million, beating the Zacks forecast of $136.7 million. For the entire year, Perion Network recorded a loss of $7.9 million on revenues of $439.9 million.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Discusses Gartner (IT) Stock
feed.summary: Jim Cramer discussed Gartner (IT) stock, noting the company's shares dropped significantly after a major selloff. Analysts at Truist and BMO Capital cut their price targets, citing a difficult market environment and contract churn. While concerns about AI's impact exist, Baron Partners Fund believes Gartner's proprietary data and insights will accelerate future contracts and are well-insulated from AI displacement.

feed.title: Rosen Law Firm Urges Enphase Energy, Inc. (NASDAQ: ENPH) Stockholders with Large Losses to Contact the Firm for Information About Their Rights
feed.summary: Rosen Law Firm has announced a class action lawsuit against Enphase Energy, Inc. (NASDAQ: ENPH) on behalf of investors who purchased securities between April 22, 2025, and October 28, 2025. The lawsuit alleges that Enphase made false and misleading statements regarding its ability to manage channel inventory and mitigate the impact of the Residential Clean Energy Credit termination. Shareholders who suffered losses are urged to contact the firm by April 20, 2026, to discuss potentially serving as lead plaintiff.

feed.title: Keurig Dr Pepper Q4 Earnings Approaching: Will It Surprise Investors?
feed.summary: Keurig Dr Pepper (KDP) is set to release its Q4 2025 earnings, with expectations of revenue growth to $4.36 billion and EPS up 1.7% to 59 cents. While strong brand performance, pricing actions, and growth in the Refreshment Beverages segment are expected to drive results, the company faces headwinds from tariff and inflationary pressures, rising SG&A costs, and a struggling Coffee segment. Based on the Zacks model, KDP is not conclusively predicted to beat earnings this quarter due to a 0.00% Earnings ESP despite a Zacks Rank of 2.

feed.title: Sherwin-Williams Declares Dividend of $0.80 per Common Share
feed.summary: The Sherwin-Williams Company has announced a regular quarterly dividend of $0.80 per common share. This dividend is payable on March 13, 2026, to shareholders of record as of March 2, 2026. This declaration marks the 47th consecutive year of dividend increases for the company.

feed.title: Expanding our CCS operations in Louisiana with another project startup
feed.summary: ExxonMobil has announced the startup of its second active commercial carbon capture and storage (CCS) operation in Louisiana, the New Generation Gas Gathering (NG3) project. This project will capture up to 1.2 million metric tons of CO2 annually from a natural gas stream. This adds to their existing CCS operation with CF Industries and highlights Louisiana's role in low-carbon industrial solutions, supporting industries like LNG and attracting new investments like data centers.

feed.title: Barclays Raises McKesson (MCK) Price Target to $1050 | MCK Stock News
feed.summary: Barclays has raised its price target for McKesson (MCK) to $1050, maintaining an "Overweight" rating, indicating a positive outlook for the company. This follows similar price target adjustments from other analysts like J.P. Morgan, Mizuho, Wells Fargo, and TD Cowen, signaling broad confidence in McKesson's performance and future prospects within the pharmaceutical wholesale industry. Despite these positive analyst forecasts, GuruFocus estimates a potential downside based on its GF Value fair value calculation.

feed.title: Kenvue Analysts Boost Their Forecasts After Upbeat Q4 Earnings
feed.summary: Kenvue (NYSE: KVUE) reported strong Q4 2025 earnings, surpassing analyst estimates with 27 cents per share and $3.780 billion in sales. This upbeat performance led to a 2.4% rise in Kenvue shares and prompted analysts from UBS and Citigroup to raise their price targets for the stock. The company's CEO, Kirk Perry, attributed the success to disciplined execution and a favorable comparison to the previous year, with a focus on enhancing performance and completing the combination with Kimberly-Clark in 2026.

feed.title: Dollar Tree opens more stores in affluent ZIP codes as prices rise
feed.summary: Dollar Tree is increasingly opening new stores in more affluent ZIP codes, with half of its new locations since 2019 being in wealthier areas. This shift reflects a strategic move to attract higher-income shoppers, especially after the company began carrying items priced above $1. Despite attracting wealthier customers, Dollar Tree emphasizes that lower-income households remain a crucial part of its customer base.

feed.title: Michael Hakes' Top Picks: Nu Holdings, Thermo Fisher Scientific & Amazon.com
feed.summary: Michael Hakes, a senior portfolio manager at Murray Wealth Group, has revealed his top stock selections for investors to consider. His chosen picks include Nu Holdings, Thermo Fisher Scientific, and Amazon.com. This article highlights these companies as key stocks to watch in the current market.

feed.title: Morning Business Report: FAA orders merit-based pilot certification as Apple expands and Wendy’s closes stores
feed.summary: The FAA has mandated that U.S. airlines certify pilots based solely on merit, moving away from DEI initiatives, a decision supported by Sean Duffy for safety reasons. Concurrently, Apple is venturing into video podcasting to compete with existing platforms, and Wendy's plans to close approximately 350 U.S. locations while expanding its value menu to combat declining sales.

feed.title: Sysco sued over alleged unlawful tobacco surcharge in employee health plans
feed.summary: Sysco is facing a class action lawsuit for allegedly imposing unlawful and discriminatory tobacco surcharges on its employee health plans. Plaintiffs Julie Avalos and Randy Wolfe claim Sysco violated ERISA by charging a biweekly "tobacco surcharge" without providing a compliant wellness program that offers a reasonable alternative standard to avoid the fee. The lawsuit argues that this practice disproportionately impacts low-income workers and undermines ERISA's intent to protect employees from health-based discrimination.

feed.title: M&T BANK CORP SEC 10-K Report
feed.summary: M&T Bank Corp has released its 2025 Form 10-K report, detailing a strong financial performance marked by increased net interest income, net income, and earnings per share. The report outlines the company's business operations, including its banking and wealth management services across multiple states and limited international activities, alongside strategic initiatives focused on loan portfolio quality, capital management, and future acquisitions. It also addresses significant challenges and risks such as market interest rate volatility, operational risks from cybersecurity threats, evolving regulatory environments, and emerging climate-related and geopolitical uncertainties.

feed.title: TRIP: B. Riley Securities Lowers Price Target for Tripadvisor |
feed.summary: B. Riley Securities has lowered its price target for Tripadvisor (TRIP) to $13.00 from $18.00, while maintaining a "Neutral" rating. This adjustment reflects a trend among several analysts who have recently revised their price targets downward for Tripadvisor. Despite these revisions, the average one-year target price from 15 analysts is $15.30, implying a 45.09% upside from its current price, and GuruFocus estimates a fair value of $21.85, suggesting a 107.21% upside.

feed.title: Exclusive - US refiners Phillips 66, Citgo seek to buy crude directly from Venezuela, sources say
feed.summary: U.S. refiners Phillips 66 and Citgo are reportedly seeking to purchase crude oil directly from Venezuela. This move comes as the U.S. has partially eased sanctions on Venezuela's oil industry. The potential direct purchases could impact global crude markets and U.S.-Venezuela relations.

feed.title: FDA Accepts BMY's NDA for Iberdomide Combo in Multiple Myeloma
feed.summary: Bristol Myers Squibb (BMY) announced that the FDA has accepted its new drug application (NDA) for iberdomide in combination with standard therapies for relapsed or refractory multiple myeloma (RRMM). The FDA granted priority review, with a decision expected by August 17, 2026, and also gave iberdomide Breakthrough Therapy designation for this indication. This proprietary drug, a novel CELMoD, aims to establish a new treatment foundation for multiple myeloma.

feed.title: Jim Cramer discusses over trading in Uber (UBER) stock
feed.summary: This article mentions that Jim Cramer discussed overtrading in Uber (UBER) stock. It implies that investors might be engaging in excessive buying or selling of UBER shares based on short-term factors rather than long-term fundamentals. Further details of Cramer's commentary are not provided in the snippet.

feed.title: NYC Pension Funds Sue AT&T Over Blocked Diversity Disclosure Proposal
feed.summary: Four New York City public pension funds have sued AT&T Inc. for unlawfully excluding a shareholder proposal that would require the telecommunications company to publicly disclose detailed workforce demographic data. The funds argue that AT&T violated federal securities law by refusing to include the proposal in its 2026 proxy materials, which seeks the public disclosure of its Consolidated EEO-1 Report, thereby depriving shareholders of a vote on a significant policy issue.

feed.title: FirstEnergy Posts Higher 2025 Profit, Launches $36 Billion Grid Investment Plan
feed.summary: FirstEnergy Corp. reported increased GAAP earnings of $1.02 billion ($1.77 per share) and consolidated revenue of $15.1 billion for full-year 2025. The company also announced a new five-year capital investment program, "Energize365," totaling $36 billion from 2026 through 2030, which includes over $19 billion for transmission investments. FirstEnergy expects this plan to drive a 10% compounded annual growth rate through 2030 and affirmed a core earnings guidance range of $2.62 to $2.82 per share for fiscal year 2026.

feed.title: Stop buying your Ryobi tools from Home Depot, here's where to buy instead
feed.summary: The article suggests that consumers looking for Ryobi tools can find better deals and often better quality at Direct Tools Outlet (DTO) rather than Home Depot. DTO, owned by Ryobi's parent company TTI, specializes in selling refurbished or used tools that undergo rigorous testing and often come with a generous warranty. While Home Depot only sells new or clearance items, DTO provides a cost-effective alternative for budget-conscious buyers, whether through their physical stores or online shop.

feed.title: C-suite changes continue at Charles River with new CFO, legal chief
feed.summary: Charles River Laboratories is undergoing significant leadership changes, appointing a new CFO and legal chief amidst declining revenue and pressure from an activist shareholder. Longtime CEO Jim Foster is also set to retire after three decades with the company. These changes come as the company seeks to address its financial performance and shareholder concerns.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Linked Broadcom (AVGO) & Computer Storage Stocks
feed.summary: Jim Cramer discussed Broadcom (AVGO) in relation to computer storage stocks like Micron, Western Digital, Seagate, and Sandisk. He suggested that if these "shortage stocks" decline, Broadcom, NVIDIA, and AMD would rally. Broadcom's stock is up 42% over the past year but down 6.5% YTD, with analysts offering mixed ratings and price targets.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Discusses Salesforce (CRM) Stock
feed.summary: Jim Cramer discussed Salesforce (NYSE:CRM) stock, highlighting a comparison with Walmart regarding cash flow and growth rates. While Salesforce's shares have seen a significant decline, Stifel reiterated a Buy rating, citing the potential of its Agentforce platform in the AI era. Piper Sandler, however, cut its price target on the stock.

feed.title: American Tower snaps after six consecutive sessions of gains
feed.summary: The article reports that American Tower (AMT) stock has experienced a dip after a streak of six consecutive sessions with gains, signaling a potential shift in its short-term performance.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Believes He Got the Last Laugh With Home Depot (HD) Stock
feed.summary: Jim Cramer expressed satisfaction with his decision to buy Home Depot (HD) stock for his charitable trust, stating that critics are "not laughing now." Analysts from Truist and TD Cowen have raised their price targets for Home Depot (HD) in January, maintaining Buy ratings due to strong card data, holiday updates, potential tax refund benefits, and its strong market positioning. Despite recent stock fluctuations, Cramer's conviction reinforces bullish sentiment among some financial professionals.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Admits He Got Emerson (EMR) Wrong
feed.summary: Jim Cramer of CNBC admitted he was wrong about Emerson Electric Co. (EMR), acknowledging that its acquisitions, which he initially opposed, actually worked. He now sees Emerson as a strong performer, likening it to Eaton, benefiting from its automation and software products, and exposure to growth markets like data centers. Analysts at JPMorgan and Evercore ISI have also expressed positive views on EMR, with Evercore setting a $170 share price target due to the company's strategic initiatives and portfolio quality improvements.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Is Happy About Chipotle (CMG)
feed.summary: Jim Cramer has expressed optimism about Chipotle Mexican Grill (CMG) following the company's decision to lower expectations, which he believes will lead to better-than-anticipated same-store sales numbers. Despite recent analyst downgrades of price targets, Cramer highlighted Chipotle's new ad campaign and specials as potential drivers for increased customer traffic. The article also touches on analyst coverage from Guggenheim and Telsey Advisory, both of whom adjusted price targets for CMG.

feed.title: Why Is Cadence Design Systems (CDNS) Stock Soaring Today
feed.summary: Cadence Design Systems (CDNS) stock jumped 9.2% today after the company reported better-than-expected fourth-quarter financial results for December 2025 and issued a strong outlook for 2026. Revenue of $1.44 billion and adjusted EPS of $1.99 both surpassed analyst estimates, and the full-year 2026 adjusted EPS guidance of $8.10 also impressed investors. This positive news signals continued demand for Cadence's electronic design automation software and hardware.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Believes Adobe’s (ADBE) In Trouble
feed.summary: Jim Cramer suggests Adobe (ADBE) is facing difficulties, particularly in the AI market, leading to a significant drop in its stock value. Goldman Sachs, UBS, and Piper Sandler have all downgraded Adobe's stock and reduced price targets due to concerns about its NTM revenue growth and EPS lagging behind competitors. Cramer believes consumers can find cheaper alternatives to Adobe's products, exacerbating the company's challenges.

feed.title: PayPal (PYPL) is Facing Stiff Competition From Apple, Believes Jim Cramer
feed.summary: Jim Cramer believes PayPal (PYPL) is facing severe competition from Apple Pay, which he considers a superior product that has "obliterated" PayPal. This comes as PayPal's shares have significantly declined over the past year and year-to-date, with analysts like Truist and RBC Capital lowering price targets due to sluggish volume growth and take rate compression. Cramer suggests PayPal needs to change its shareholder base and develop new offerings to stop the bleeding against Apple's strong payment solution.

feed.title: Insulet (PODD) Shares Skyrocket, What You Need To Know
feed.summary: Insulet Corporation (PODD) shares jumped 5.6% after the company announced strong fourth-quarter 2025 financial results, beating Wall Street expectations for revenue and adjusted earnings per share. The company reported a 31.2% increase in revenue year-over-year and provided a positive outlook with a guided 26% year-over-year sales increase for Q1 2026. Despite a slight dip year-to-date, the market views the news as meaningful, indicating strong demand for its Omnipod insulin management systems.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Discusses Eli Lilly (LLY) & Weight Loss Drugs
feed.summary: Jim Cramer highlighted Eli Lilly (LLY) and its strong performance in the weight loss drug market, noting its lead over Novo Nordisk. The company reported impressive Q4 and FY2025 earnings, leading to upgraded price targets from Freedom Capital and Deutsche Bank. Cramer specifically mentioned the success of Eli Lilly's weight loss pill, which is gaining popularity due to its ease of use compared to injections.

feed.title: TC Energy Forecasts Natural Gas Demand Surge Equivalent to Entire European Market
feed.summary: TC Energy projects a significant increase in North American natural gas demand, estimating a jump of 45 Bcf/d by 2035. This surge is primarily attributed to rising LNG exports, increased power generation needs, and the growing demand for reliability from distribution companies. The forecast highlights natural gas's critical role in meeting future energy requirements across North America.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Discusses “Over Trading” In Uber (UBER) Stock
feed.summary: Jim Cramer commented on the "over trading" in Uber (UBER) stock, suggesting investors should focus on long-term investing rather than short-term price fluctuations. Despite a recent dip in share price, Uber's gross bookings and revenue exceeded analyst estimates. Aristotle Capital Value Equity Strategy also discussed Uber, acknowledging near-term investor concerns but maintaining a positive long-term outlook due to the company's global scale and growing free cash flow.

feed.title: Riot Platforms Pushed by Activist Investor to Speed Up AI Transition. The Stock Rises.
feed.summary: Activist investor Starboard Value is pushing Riot Platforms to accelerate its transition to artificial intelligence data centers. While Riot Platforms, like other crypto miners, has begun this shift, Starboard Value believes the company needs to move more quickly to capitalize on the AI trend.

feed.title: Vulcan Materials Co (NYSE:VMC) Lags Estimates
feed.summary: Vulcan Materials Co (NYSE:VMC) reported fourth-quarter earnings below analyst expectations, with a 9% decline in gross profit. Despite the quarterly setback, the company achieved full-year net earnings growth of 18% and a 7% increase in annual revenue. Looking to 2026, Vulcan anticipates continued strength in public construction and improving private nonresidential opportunities, projecting adjusted EBITDA between $2.4 billion and $2.6 billion.

feed.title: Republic Services Inc (NYSE:RSG) Posts Mixed Result
feed.summary: Republic Services Inc (NYSE:RSG) stock declined 4.67% after reporting mixed results for the fourth quarter of FY25. While net income and adjusted EPS surpassed analyst estimates, revenue growth of 2.2% to $4.14 billion missed expectations due to organic decline in environmental solutions and a decrease in recycled commodity prices. Despite mixed performance, the company projects revenue between $17.050 billion and $17.150 billion for fiscal year 2026, with adjusted diluted EPS expected to be $7.20 to $7.28.

feed.title: Medtronic PLC (NYSE:MDT) Margin Contracts
feed.summary: Medtronic PLC (NYSE:MDT) reported strong third-quarter fiscal 2026 results, exceeding analyst expectations with significant revenue growth across its portfolios, especially in Cardiovascular. Despite overall strong performance and beating EPS and revenue estimates, the company's gross margin and adjusted operating margin contracted due to increased cost of products sold. Medtronic maintained its fiscal year 2026 guidance, expecting continued organic revenue growth and stable adjusted EPS.

feed.title: Newton Golf adds finance veteran to board
feed.summary: Newton Golf Company has appointed John Bode to its board of directors, increasing the number of independent directors to three. Bode brings extensive financial oversight from public companies and direct golf industry experience, including owning three golf clubs and founding Maxim Golf Solutions. This appointment follows a record revenue performance in 2025 for Newton Golf, which is focused on product line and international expansion.

feed.title: EQT Corp (NYSE:EQT) Posts Strong Production
feed.summary: EQT Corp reported strong fourth-quarter earnings and revenue, surpassing analyst expectations due to increased production, improved pricing, and reduced costs. The company's sales volumes and free cash flow were robust, and it beat EPS and revenue estimates. EQT also provided a positive outlook for 2026, forecasting strong production and significant free cash flow.

feed.title: Devon Energy Corp ( NYSE:DVN) Gave Downbeat Guidance
feed.summary: Devon Energy Corp (NYSE:DVN) stock rose slightly after beating fourth-quarter profit estimates, but forecast lower first-quarter production due to severe winter storms. The company recently announced a $58 billion merger with Coterra Energy, aiming for $1 billion in annual pre-tax savings by 2027. Despite the production forecast, Devon reported increased fourth-quarter production and improvements in its financial position, including reduced long-term debt and beating revenue expectations.

feed.title: Cadence Design Systems Inc (NASDAQ:CDNS) Posts Record Backlog
feed.summary: Cadence Design Systems Inc. (NASDAQ: CDNS) reported better-than-expected Q4 FY25 results, with its stock rising 9.44%. The company finished 2025 with a record backlog of $7.8 billion and announced collaborations with TSMC and Intel Foundry. Cadence's core EDA business, hardware business, IP business, and System Design and Analysis business all showed strong growth, and the company provided an optimistic outlook for fiscal 2026.

feed.title: Coca-Cola investment in province creating 500 jobs
feed.summary: Coca-Cola Canada Bottling Ltd. is investing $141 million to expand its Brampton, Ont. facility, which is projected to create up to 500 construction jobs and enhance Ontario's manufacturing and agri-food sectors. The project will add a new advanced production line, bolstering the province's supply chain capacity and reinforcing its economic strength. Ontario officials, including Premier Doug Ford, lauded the investment as a significant endorsement of the province's workforce and economic policies.

feed.title: Caesars launches Ca$hline slot game across online casino platforms
feed.summary: Caesars Entertainment (NASDAQ:CZR) has introduced a new three-reel slot game called Ca$hline on its online casino platforms in New Jersey, developed by its in-house studio, Empire Creative. The game offers flexible gameplay with features like a 2x multiplier and a wheel bonus. Despite a significant stock price decline, InvestingPro analysis suggests the stock is currently undervalued, following a mixed Q4 earnings report with a wider-than-expected loss but increased revenue, driven by strong digital performance.

feed.title: Northern Trust names Jessica Donohue as head of Asset Servicing, Americas
feed.summary: Northern Trust has appointed Jessica Donohue as head of Asset Servicing, Americas. Donohue previously served as global head of product management for Asset Servicing and held senior leadership positions at State Street. The financial services firm has seen its shares climb 32.92% over the past year and reported strong Q4 2025 earnings.

feed.title: Assessing Southwest Airlines (LUV) Valuation After A Strong Multi‑Period Share Price Rally
feed.summary: This article analyzes Southwest Airlines (LUV) valuation after significant share price gains, noting a discrepancy between a popular narrative estimating the company as 14.3% overvalued at $54.26, and a DCF model suggesting a 59.9% discount. The narrative anticipates strong earnings growth from loyalty program optimization and partnership agreements, while the DCF model projects a fair value of $135.47 per share. The article encourages investors to examine the underlying assumptions to form their own conclusions.

feed.title: New Charles River Leaders May Shape Valuation, Debt And Governance Outlook
feed.summary: Charles River Laboratories International (NYSE:CRL) has appointed Glenn G. Coleman as Chief Financial Officer and Kerry Dailey as Chief Legal Officer. These new leadership roles are expected to influence the company's financial oversight, capital allocation, debt management, and regulatory compliance, particularly given the company's current high debt level. Investors will be watching how these executives shape decision-making and report on these matters in future communications.

feed.title: What's Going On With ParaZero Technologies Stock Wednesday? - ParaZero Technologies (NASDAQ:PRZO)
feed.summary: ParaZero Technologies (NASDAQ: PRZO) stock rose on Wednesday after the company announced a significant breakthrough in drone-interception technology. Its DefendAir system successfully intercepted a high-speed drone traveling at 70 mph, marking the first-ever successful net-based interception at such a speed. Despite this positive development, the company has faced challenges over the past year, with its stock price declining and trading below key moving averages, indicating a cautious long-term outlook.

feed.title: EQT buys larger stake in Mountain Valley Pipeline
feed.summary: EQT, in a joint venture with Blackstone Credit and Insurance, is set to acquire Con Edison's stake in a pipeline for $213 million. This transaction involves exercising an option to buy compression and mainline assets, and is expected to close this year. The article highlights EQT's increased investment in the Mountain Valley Pipeline.
